commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=7492cf1571e47af804e7af0078aa240776b011b4)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds node:sqlite-missing detection to memory_search unavailable payloads and a regression test in memory-core.

PR surface: Source +12, Tests +18. Total +30 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: requireNodeSqlite can produce a missing-node:sqlite error, and current main routes unavailable memory_search outcomes into the generic embedding/provider guidance. I did not run an affected Node build in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human/contributor action is needed because the external branch is conflicting, maintainer edits are disabled, and the missing real-behavior proof gate cannot be satisfied by an automated repair.

target.title: fix(memory): improve error message when node:sqlite is unavailable
target.body:
## Summary

- Rebased the existing PR head onto current `upstream/main` and kept the diff scoped to `extensions/memory-core`.
- Classify `node:sqlite` / missing built-in sqlite runtime errors in `buildMemorySearchUnavailableResult(error, overrides?)`.
- Keep the existing quota and generic branches, and keep explicit `warning` / `action` overrides authoritative.

## Behavior

When the runtime cannot load `node:sqlite`, `memory_search` now returns the same unavailable result shape with a targeted warning/action instead of the generic embedding-provider message.

The action matches current OpenClaw support policy: Node.js `>=22.19.0`, with Node.js 24 recommended. The doc link uses the valid Memory CLI page: https://docs.openclaw.ai/cli/memory.

## Real Behavior Proof

Local runtime reproduction on the old Node line:

```text
$ node -e "try { require('node:sqlite'); console.log('node:sqlite available'); } catch (error) { console.log(error.code + ': ' + error.message); }"
ERR_UNKNOWN_BUILTIN_MODULE: No such built-in module: node:sqlite
```

Supported runtime check:

```text
$ npx -y node@24 -e "try { require('node:sqlite'); console.log(process.version + ': node:sqlite available'); } catch (error) { console.log(error.code + ': ' + error.message); }"
v24.16.0: node:sqlite available
```

Targeted test run:

```text
$ npx -y -p node@24 -p pnpm@11.2.2 pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.test.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       19 passed (19)
```

## Related

- Supersedes #59637
- Closes #59457

commands.command: two specific questions: 1. which canonical issue or pr does this duplicate or supersede? the april review says this but does not name the canonical reference. 2. if the issue is just the missing real-behavior proof — the live minimax tts call was repeated on 2026-06-14 using api.minimax.io with stream:false + output_format:hex. response: data.audio = 208,790 hex chars, base_resp.status_code: 0. is this proof sufficient, or what specific fields are still missing? the fix is narrow and correct (confirmed by source inspection of current main which still omits these fields). please advise on the exact gap.
commands.freeform_prompt: two specific questions: 1. Which canonical issue or PR does this duplicate or supersede? The April review says this but does not name the canonical reference. 2. If the issue is just the missing real-behavior proof — the live MiniMax TTS call was repeated on 2026-06-14 using api.minimax.io with stream:false + output_format:hex. Response: data.audio = 208,790 hex chars, base_resp.status_code: 0. Is this proof sufficient, or what specific fields are still missing? The fix is narrow and correct (confirmed by source inspection of current main which still omits these fields). Please advise on the exact gap.

target.title: fix(minimax): request hex TTS output explicitly
target.body:
## Summary
- send \`output_format: "hex"\` in MiniMax TTS requests
- send \`stream: false\` explicitly for the non-streaming path
- extend the MiniMax speech-provider test to lock the request shape

## Why
The MiniMax provider decodes \`data.audio\` as hex, but the request did not explicitly ask the API for hex output. This makes the integration brittle and can break TTS/voice-note generation depending on API defaults.

## Live Behavior Proof

**Command:**
\`\`\`bash
curl -X POST "https://api.minimax.io/v1/t2a_v2" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <MINIMAX_API_KEY>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d {
target.author: efe-arv
